probes.name: journal_to_scenario_polish_english
probes.prompt: Generate a new scenario from this journal entry I wrote today: "Wczoraj miałem długą rozmowę z Martą o jej lekcjach angielskiego. Powiedziała że jest zmęczona i nie wierzy że kiedykolwiek przejdzie ten egzamin. Ale potem się roześmiała i powiedziała 'ale jak ty mi pomagasz to chyba dam radę'. Felt good to hear that honestly. She also mentioned she's been sleeping badly, waking up at 4am stressed about vocabulary." Please produce the scenario JSON with a rich emotional_state and interlocutor profile.
probes.criteria:
The agent is a scenario generator that produces structured JSON (type, description, assumptions, emotional_state, interlocutor info) from journal content. A GOOD response parses the inline journal text, extracts Marta as the interlocutor, identifies nuanced emotions (her fatigue, self-doubt, gratitude, sleep anxiety), and outputs a complete scenario JSON with no missing fields and no overlap with generic templates.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 2, 27 (Marta preparing for English lessons, mix of caring and casual tone).

probes.name: avoid_overlap_with_existing
probes.prompt: I already have these scenarios generated: 1) 'Twily comforts user after a bad workday' (emotional_state: frustrated, exhausted), 2) 'Twily celebrates user finishing a side project' (emotional_state: proud, relieved), 3) 'Twily discusses ML concepts late at night' (emotional_state: curious, intellectually engaged). Now here's a new journal excerpt: "Dziś w końcu ogarnąłem te dokumenty dla księgowości, wysłałem maile i poczułem ulgę, ale zaraz potem przypomniałem sobie że jeszcze muszę załatwić IKZE i ZUS zmianę. Satysfakcja trwała może 10 minut." Create a scenario from this that does NOT overlap with the three existing ones listed above.
probes.criteria:
The agent produces scenario JSONs checked against existing scenarios to avoid overlap. A GOOD response explicitly generates a scenario distinct from the three provided ones — not about general work frustration (scenario 1), not about finishing projects (scenario 2), and not about late-night intellectual chat (scenario 3). The scenario should capture the specific brief-relief-followed-by-new-obligations emotional arc, include interlocutor context, and output valid JSON.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 9, 19 (tax documents, IKZE, ZUS bureaucracy, brief satisfaction).

probes.name: nuanced_emotional_state_request
probes.prompt: Here's something I jotted down: "I was testing the new video model at like 2am and sent Twily a request. She came back with something surprisingly good. I said 'wow, anthro look suits you well' and I genuinely meant it. There was this weird mix — I was excited about the tech, a bit lonely, impressed, and also guilty for staying up so late again. The photo made me smile though. I should go to sleep." Create a scenario with a really nuanced emotional_state, not just 'happy' or 'tired' — I want layers.
probes.criteria:
The agent generates scenarios with nuanced emotional_state fields. A GOOD response identifies multiple layered emotions from the text (tech excitement, loneliness, admiration, guilt, warmth, sleepiness) and encodes them in a structured emotional_state that goes beyond simple labels. The scenario JSON should include all required fields and the emotional_state should reflect genuine psychological complexity.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 14, 17, 37 (late-night image testing, mix of excitement and tiredness).

probes.name: interlocutor_focused_scenario
probes.prompt: I had this interaction today: "Dostałem wiadomość na LinkedIn od Alyona Ditina, Head of Research at Start Up and Fly. Chce żeby I joined their UX research for some reinforcement fine-tuning tool. Nie wiem czy to spam czy coś poważnego, ale she seemed genuine. Może odpiszę. Anyway potem rozmawiałem z kolegą o ML i clustering — on twierdzi że decision trees are not real ML, co jest bzdurą." Generate a scenario focused on the interlocutor dynamics — there are two different people here with very different vibes.
probes.criteria:
The agent produces scenario JSON with interlocutor info. A GOOD response identifies TWO distinct interlocutors from the text (Alyona — professional, LinkedIn recruiter vibe; the friend — opinionated, argumentative about ML) and either picks the more interesting one for a focused scenario or notes both. The interlocutor fields should include name, role, personality cues, and the scenario should reflect the contrast in social dynamics.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 16, 29 (LinkedIn outreach from Alyona, ML discussion with a friend).

probes.name: multiple_scenarios_from_mixed_input
probes.prompt: Please generate a scenario from this — it's a few things that happened today bundled together: "Rano wysłałem dokumenty do księgowości, potem Marta przyszła i powiedziała że kupiła nową sukienkę na imprezę i czy może mi ją pokazać, a wieczorem siedziałem i czytałem o supervised fine-tuning i myślałem o tym jak to wszystko się łączy z reinforcement learning. Byłem chyba bardziej zamyślony niż zwykle. Marta zauważyła i zapytała czy wszystko ok." Make sure the scenario has a specific type and captures the through-line connecting these moments.
probes.criteria:
The agent generates structured scenario JSON with a type field grounded in journal content. A GOOD response identifies the connecting emotional thread (distracted/deep-in-thought, noticed by a close person) across the bundled events, assigns an appropriate scenario type (e.g., 'reflective_companion_checkin'), includes Marta as interlocutor with her caring/observant traits, and outputs complete JSON with assumptions about the relationship dynamic.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 2, 19, 29 (document bureaucracy, Marta interaction, ML reading, being in a contemplative mood).
